Does the Act allow a non-candidate-controlled committee to retllrn remaining campaign

The Act allows a committee several options with its remaining funds, including clonating

"Meastlre A.'' The measure was defeated and the PAC has no plans to sllpport any other
measLlres' The only contributors to the PAC's fund were members of the sheriff,s Association
who contributed directly through their paychecks. Now that the measure has been defeated and
the PAC is terminating, you would like to move the remaining pAC funds to the Association,s
general fund.
ANALYSIS
Your question, regarding a committee not controlled by a candidate, involves funds that
are remaining after an election. Section 89512.5 states that, bicause the money in these
committees is "held in trust,"2 expenditures of excess funds must be ,.reasonably related to a
political, Iegislative, or governmental purpo se of the committee.,, (Regulation g9512.5(a),
emphasis added') The underlying theorylor this rule is that these funJs are held in tmst by the
committee for certain limited usei.

ln a similar situation, we have found.that "refunding" committee money to the organization
that began the committee is not "reasonably related to ipolitical, legislative, or governmental
purpose of the committee." (See pappyAdvice Letter, e-g+-o:r.) when a committee
terminated and had leftover funds torefund, rro*"u"r, *o wanted to return them to a major
donor that was also a non-profit corporation and wouid use the funds for the specific purpose for
which the committee was organized to advocate, *. fo,,nJ that the expenditure was for a
political' legislative, ff Eovemqental purpose. (see pirayouAdvice Letter, A-0g-143.) Because
the organization would use the funds in a way that was consistent with the committee,s mission,
the return of funds was appropriate.3
under your facts, the actual donors are the members of the sheriff s Association, rather than
the sheriff's Association itself. You also stated that there is no intention to support a similar
measure' Retuming the funds to the sheriff s Association would comply with Section g9512.5 if

restricted to a political or legislativ" ur" tho, is in harmt.,y *i,r,,r," purpose of
You have other options. regarding your excess funds. These include retuming the funds to
the actual donors (the Sheriff i Atro-"iotion members; lsee r;roy ott, supra)or donating to a non-
profit (see Section 89515).
Please note that Elections code section 18680 (copy encrosed) imposes duties on those
entrusted with funds directed at the promotion of balloimeirur.r. Additionally, section g9522 of
the Act states that chapter 9, concerning use of campaign funds, ,.shall not be construed to
2 "All contributions deposited into the campaign account shall be deemed to be held in trust fbr expenses
associated with the election of the candidate or for .ipJnr", .r;";i.;; with hording omce: ii..tion g95 r0(b).)
Section 895 12.5(a) states that Section 895 l0(b) appriis to non-candidate controlled commitrees.
I rhe organization would use the firnds to advocate for an e.rpansion of a convention center, which was the
same goal that the measure supported.

permit an expenditure of campaign funds prohibited by Section lg6g0 of the Elections code.,,
The advice given in this letteipresumes compliance with the obligations of section lg6g0.

sections.question: Does the Act allow a non-candidate-controlled committee to return remaining campaign funds to the sponsoring organization's general fund?
sections.conclusion: The Act allows a committee several options with its remaining funds, including donating the money to a charitable organization, refunding contributors, or making expenditures for other political, legislative, or governmental purposes. The expenditure can be made to the sponsoring organization's general fund only in limited circumstances.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act permits a non-candidate-controlled political action committee to return remaining funds to its sponsoring organization's general fund after the measure it supported was defeated.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Returning funds to the sponsoring organization's general fund is permitted under Section 89512.5 only if the funds are restricted to political or legislative uses consistent with the committee's original purpose. The organization has alternative options including refunding actual donors or donating to nonprofits.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; Skipped (too short): conclusion, question; QUESTION has only 0 words; CONCLUSION has only 0 words; LLM: Document contains complete QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections despite parsing notes suggesting they were empty. The FACTS, ANALYSIS, and complete CONCLUSION sections were successfully extracted. File No. A-10-150, dated October 26, 2010.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Act allow a non-candidate-controlled committee to return remaining campaign funds to the sponsoring organization's general fund?

CONCLUSION: The Act allows a committee several options with its remaining funds, including donating the money to a charitable organization, refunding contributors, or making expenditures for other political, legislative, or governmental purposes. The expenditure can be made to the sponsoring organization's general fund only in limited circumstances.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises a county deputy sheriffs association treasurer that its terminated political action committee may return remaining funds to the association's general fund only if restricted to political or legislative purposes aligned with the committee's mission, and provides alternative options for handling the excess funds.
